compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
Plan-review re-run at HEAD b76dd09a1 → PASS (0 blocking, 15 advisory). Triage of advisories before implementation:

- [MAJOR, honored in WS2] 'cached boolean' vs 'name permanently-failing units' inconsistency: the per-process pending cache will hold the SET of pending ids (registry_ids − parsed .ensure-applied), not a bool, so the rate-limited hint can name specific pending/failing units. ≤1 read/proc preserved (set computed once, cached).
- [minor, WS3] MCP-startup lock-held behavior: run_ensures at boot uses a short write-lock timeout and log-and-continues on LockTimeout/any error — never blocks or aborts boot.
- [minor, noted] fresh-init lock nesting is safe: _acquire_init_lock is the ticket-init.lock MKDIR lock; run_ensures takes the distinct .ticket-write.lock store write lock — no self-deadlock.
- [minor, WS1] units appear on BOTH re-init (209-213) and fresh-init (242-248): run_ensures replaces the hand-listed calls on both paths + symlink-init env-id callers; a registry-id frozen-set test guards against double-execution/rename.
- [minor, WS1/WS5] zero-commit idempotency test overlap: authoritative git-log idempotency test lives in WS5; WS1's test asserts registry shape + init wiring.
- [minor→none, WS4] docs/migrations.md will additionally cover the [ensure] config section (hint_interval_secs/hint_enabled) and the rationale for excluding inline-committer paths (compact/txn/transition_close/delete).
- [none, WS1/WS4] post-rollback: committed .gitattributes/.gitignore content stays on the tickets branch (harmless/idempotent); git-ignored .ensure-applied/.ensure-hinted are ignored by old code.
- [minor, WS1] init callers do not currently rely on ensure-step exceptions; run_ensures skip-and-continues and init logs failures, never aborts.

ac-quality (compound 'and' criteria) acknowledged as cosmetic; ACs left as-is (still independently verifiable in practice).

compiled_state.description:
## Context
rebar has no general mechanism to apply an idempotent setup/config change to an
ALREADY-EXISTING install. Init-time `_migrate_*`/`_ensure_*` functions (e.g.
`_migrate_gc_config`) run only at `init`/re-init, so a fix shipped after a store was
initialized never reaches it unless someone re-inits — the gap the `gc.auto=0` legacy-store
issue exposed. Discovered from the gc discussion (e3d0).

**School B (desired-state / convergent), not School A (ordered ledger).** Idempotent,
safe-to-re-run, drift-correcting (Ansible/Puppet/Chef check-then-act `changed`/`ok`; K8s
level-triggered reconcile). We do NOT build an ordered version ledger (Alembic/Flyway/Rails
are for irreversible, unsafe-to-rerun migrations). The A-tier ledger (committed store-format
version, ordered, fail-closed on too-new) is future work (docs/migrations.md + doctor idea dabb).

## Design (corrected against origin/main via two plan-review passes)
**Ensure-registry (`ensures.py`).** A registry of ensure units, each `{stable, immutable id,
callable(tracker) -> EnsureOutcome}` where the callable is check-then-act (no-op when
converged) and `EnsureOutcome` is a small dataclass `{id, status: Literal["ok","changed",
"failed"], detail}`. `run_ensures(tracker)` runs ALL units unconditionally under the STORE
write lock (`_store/lock.py` write_lock — init_core does NOT currently hold it), catches a
raising unit (skip-and-continue → `failed`), returns the outcome list, and rewrites
`.ensure-applied` to the ids of the NON-failed (ok/changed) units only.

**The exact 5 re-init idempotent steps migrated** (init.py:209-213 — the already-initialized
path that reaches existing stores): `env-id`, `gc-config`, `merge-ours driver`,
`gitattributes` (COMMIT), `gitignore` (COMMIT). **Two units commit** (gitattributes AND
gitignore) → check-then-act (tree-check before commit) required for BOTH. These same units
also appear on the fresh-init path (init.py:242-248) and `_ensure_env_id` is additionally
called from `_init_via_symlink` (init.py:564, 582); the migration wires `run_ensures()` at all
init entry points and removes the hand-listed calls from each, with no double-execution. The
fresh-init-only bootstrap (`_commit_precommit`, `_gen_local_files`, `_exclude_scratch_in_tracker`,
`_ensure_branch_user_config`) are NOT ensures and stay.

**Entry points (non-hot):** init/re-init/symlink-init (always), `rebar fsck --repair` (opt-in),
MCP server startup (best-effort — log-and-continue, never abort boot; under write lock). The
sweep never runs on the hot write path or on pure reads.

**fsck reconciliation (origin/main ALREADY has `fsck --repair`).** `fsck --repair` is the
existing A3 data-remediation verb (rebuild snapshots, retire orphans; requires mutation;
`--dry-run`/`--limit`). We do NOT add a new `--fix` flag — we FOLD the ensure-sweep into the
existing `--repair` path (one "drive to healthy" verb, both data + config). Plain read-only
`rebar fsck` gains an `ensures: N/M applied` line derived WITHOUT calling run_ensures: M =
count of registry ids, N = count present in `.ensure-applied`. The pending-hint points to
`rebar fsck --repair`.

**Concurrency.** run_ensures runs under the store write lock so concurrent sweeps serialize;
check-then-act makes the second sweep a no-op. gitattributes/gitignore use `merge=ours`.
`.ensure-applied` is a full atomic rewrite (temp+rename) of the current non-failed id set — no
read-modify-write.

**Applied-set marker + write-path pending-hint (Rails CheckPending, hardened).**
- `.ensure-applied` (WS1-owned: format+write) and `.ensure-hinted` (WS2-owned) — both git-ignored
  (ADD BOTH to `_GITIGNORE`, init.py:38-48), atomic; `.ensure-applied` = JSON array, absent/garbage
  → empty set; `.ensure-hinted` = last-hinted timestamp.
- Hook the COMMON mutation paths via `_seam.append_event` / `event_append.write_and_push`
  (create/edit/link/comment/tag/set_*/sign/transition/claim), lazy import. Best-effort, NOT
  universal: `compact.py`, `txn.py`/`transition_close.py`, `delete.py` commit inline and are
  intentionally NOT hooked (acceptable — a rate-limited nudge, not correctness).
- `pending = registry ids − parsed .ensure-applied`, computed ONCE per process (cached boolean;
  registry static in a process). Converged store adds ≤1 read/process; reads untouched.
- Emit a WARNING → `rebar fsck --repair` only when pending AND `.ensure-hinted` older than the
  interval; then atomically update it. Interval + suppression are typed config (`EnsureConfig`
  in `_config_schema.py`: `ensure.hint_interval_secs`=86400, `ensure.hint_enabled`=true), Interval + suppression live in the `[ensure]` config section (`ensure.hint_interval_secs`=86400, `ensure.hint_enabled`=true); the env override is auto-derived per rebar's standard mechanism — no edits to any generated file.
- Hook swallows ALL its own exceptions incl. lazy-import ImportError/SyntaxError (debug log) — a
  write NEVER fails on the nag. Rare double-hint under concurrency accepted.
- Marker gates the HINT ONLY, never the repair. Fresh auto-init'd worktree converges at init → never hints.

**Accepted trade-offs:** long-lived MCP server converges at boot, does not re-sweep mid-life
(drift caught at next `fsck --repair`) — WS4 documents it; a permanently-failing unit stays out
of the set and is named in the rate-limited hint. Post-rollback: old code simply ignores the
git-ignored `.ensure-applied`/`.ensure-hinted` files (no schema coupling).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ensures.py` registry generalizes the 5 re-init units; each is check-then-act with a stable/immutable id + `EnsureOutcome` result; BOTH commit units (gitattributes, gitignore) tree-check (second run → zero commits)
- [ ] `run_ensures()` runs unconditionally under the store write lock at all init entry points, `fsck --repair`, and MCP startup (best-effort at startup); NEVER gated on the marker
- [ ] Migration removes the hand-listed calls from BOTH re-init and fresh-init paths (and handles `_ensure_env_id`'s `_init_via_symlink` callers) with no double-execution
- [ ] The ensure-sweep is folded into the EXISTING `fsck --repair` (no new flag); plain `fsck` prints `ensures: N/M applied` read-only (M=registry ids, N=in `.ensure-applied`, without calling run_ensures)
- [ ] `fsck --repair` heals and is git-level idempotent (twice → zero new commits); the pending-hint points to `rebar fsck --repair`
- [ ] Best-effort write-path pending-hint on the common mutation paths (inline committers acknowledged out); pure reads untouched; ≤1 read/process; never fails a write (incl. lazy-import errors)
- [ ] Hint rate-limited via `.ensure-hinted` + typed-config interval (86400s) + `[ensure]` config-section suppression (`ensure.hint_enabled`; env auto-derived); fresh auto-init'd worktree never hints
- [ ] `.ensure-applied` (JSON array, atomic full rewrite of non-failed ids) + `.ensure-hinted` are added to `_GITIGNORE`; absent/garbage → empty set (no crash)
- [ ] `run_ensures()` has a typed `EnsureOutcome` + partial-failure contract (skip-and-continue; init logs failures, never aborts)
- [ ] Hot-path regression test: mutation ops may hint but never invoke `run_ensures` (no git-config subprocess; ≤1 marker read/process)
- [ ] `docs/migrations.md` documents reuse, marker-gates-hint-never-repair, hot-path contract, long-lived-MCP trade-off, post-rollback file-ignore, and A-tier future; gc-config is the reference unit
compiled_state.title: First-class migration pattern: idempotent per-environment ensure-registry (B tier)
